name: UNLV shares resources for everyone during Sexual Assault Awareness Month
summary: UNLV students and faculty are advised to seek out help and other resources after a sexual assault on the north end of the Maryland Parkway campus.  The accused perpetrator of the attack has been apprehended and will be held without bail. Interim President Chris Heavey shared a statement with the university community following the attack: […] The post UNLV shares resources for everyone during Sexual Assault Awareness Month first appeared on UNLV Scarlet and Gray . name: “Nameless” ties spell trouble for UNLV students
summary: Students associated with the off-campus group “Nameless” are facing suspension and expulsion.  Allegedly, “Nameless” was formed from former members of the Kappa Sigma fraternity after it was disbanded following the death of UNLV student Nathan Tyler Valencia during a “fight night.”  UNLV provides the following report of that night on their Organizational Conduct website page, […] The post “Nameless” ties spell trouble for UNLV students first appeared on UNLV Scarlet and Gray . name: Las Vegas Women’s Marketing Collective partners with UNLV for internship opportunities
summary: The Las Vegas Women’s Marketing Collective (LVWMC) partnered with UNLV’s Lee Business School to connect students with local businesses at a speed-networking event. The LVWMC launched in 2024 and aims to elevate the next generation of women into their careers by providing guidance and connections in the business industry while also building a female-led community. name: IMPACTS plans for the future as the election wraps up
summary: With CSUN executive elections over, the IMPACTS ticket won with an 11.6% lead in the polls. The team’s message emphasized transparency and face-to-face communication, aiming to build trust and confidence within the student body. The Scarlet and Gray asked IMPACTS about the election results, how they’re feeling afterward and IMPACTS’ plan for the upcoming term. name: Sexual Assault on Campus
summary: The suspect accused of sexually assaulting a UNLV student after breaking into her apartment will be held without bail, a judge ruled on April 17. The suspect, John Grady, 36, was arrested just after midnight on April 15, after a joint investigation by UNLV’s Police Department (UPD) and Las Vegas Metropolitan Department (Metro). name: Women’s voices are breaking into the wrestling fandom
summary: WrestleMania 42 is coming to Las Vegas this weekend, featuring the women’s championship match between Stephanie Vaquer and Liv Morgan.  There seems to be a lack of representation for women and a lack of camaraderie. summary: UNLV Interim President Chris Heavey delivered his first State of the University address. name: William S. Boyd School of Law introduces first Native American endowed scholarship
summary: The William S. Boyd School of Law is establishing a scholarship titled the “Tribal Leadership Council Endowment” to support the next generation of Native American attorneys. The Indian Nations Gaming and Governance (INGG) Program at Boyd Law is creating the scholarship through collaboration with the Tribal Leadership Council (TLC). name: The Scarlet and Gray needs you
summary: The Scarlet and Gray is UNLV’s newspaper and is currently looking for those interested in joining its ranks.  The paper consists of five different sections: Culture, Metro, News, Opinion, and Sports. Culture covers artistic and music events taking place around the city and on campus. Metro covers any news in the Las Vegas area.
